jdBreakdown.roleSummary: This is a specialized AI evaluation science role for building production-grade LLM and AI-agent evaluation infrastructure, metrics, benchmarks, statistical experiments, safety checks, and CI/CD quality gates. It is closer to ML evaluation scientist or research engineer than general AI operations or chatbot QA.
mustHaveRequirements: 3+ years in ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Research Engineer, or ML/AI evaluation-related roles
Research or implementation experience in ML model evaluation or LLM evaluation
Deep knowledge of LLM evaluation methods, benchmark design, LLM-as-Judge, quantitative quality measurement, and hallucination detection
Statistics and experimental design knowledge including hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals, and effect sizes
Python ML or evaluation pipeline building experience
Practical experience with PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow
Ability to operate in Japanese product-development discussion or business-level English
Master's degree or equivalent practical experience in CS, ML, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Psychometrics, or related field
strongAdvantageRequirements: Reward modeling, preference learning, RLHF, or DPO
AI safety, Responsible AI, red teaming, adversarial testing, and policy compliance verification
Benchmark validity work such as IRT, construct validity, contamination detection, and proper scoring rules
Multi-agent, tool-use, multi-turn, and long-context evaluation
Large-scale data processing such as Spark or BigQuery
niceToHaveRequirements: LangSmith, Weights & Biases, pytest, GCP, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, GitHub Actions
React, Next.js, TypeScript, NX, Slack, Confluence, Linear, Notion, Claude Code, Cursor, ChatGPT, Devin
coreResponsibilities: Design and operate AI-agent quality evaluation infrastructure
Research and implement LLM evaluation metrics, benchmarks, rubrics, and calibration methods
Build automated evaluation pipelines and production quality gates
Create evaluation harnesses for multi-turn, tool-use, and long-context agents
Run statistical experiments and quality trend analysis
Build safety, red-team, hallucination, and policy compliance checks
Report quality findings and feed signals back to research and development teams

communication: cross-functional feedback to research and development teams
English technical communication or Japanese product discussion ability

senioritySignals: Lead design, construction, and operation of evaluation infrastructure
Greenfield evaluation methodology and infrastructure ownership
Production quality accountability for products used by approximately 200 companies
Ability to research, validate, maintain, and operationalize evaluation methods
Cross-functional influence on research and development teams
hiddenHiringPriorities: They need someone who can turn ambiguous AI quality risk into measurable release gates
They likely screen for real ML/statistics depth before general AI enthusiasm
They need production evaluation infrastructure, not manual QA or business analysis
They will value candidates who can prove rigor in metrics, validity, reproducibility, and statistical interpretation
They may reject candidates who cannot code evaluation pipelines in Python or discuss ML frameworks credibly

positioning.primaryHiringProblem: JAPAN AI needs a technical evaluation scientist who can build rigorous, scalable, statistically valid LLM and agent quality infrastructure for enterprise AI products.
positioning.managerHireReason: The candidate can show practical AI quality operations, chatbot evaluation, A/B attribution, GPT scoring workflows, human-review loops, governance routing, and production AI chatbot operations, but does not fully meet the ML research, Python pipeline, statistics, or ML framework depth implied by the role.
positioning.toolApplicationAngle: Position as an AI quality operations and evaluation workflow builder who has created practical GPT/chatbot evaluation loops, benchmark-style tests, A/B metrics, and governance controls for enterprise workflows. Do not position as an ML evaluation scientist unless stronger Python, statistics, and ML framework proof is added.
positioningRationale: The JD is centered on LLM/agent evaluation science, not general business automation
Candidate evidence supports practical AI evaluation operations through chatbot benchmarks, GPT scoring, A/B routing, and human review loops
Candidate evidence is weak for ML research, PyTorch/JAX/TensorFlow, reward modeling, CI/CD evaluation gates, and advanced statistical testing
The safest angle is adjacent AI Evaluation Ops, not pure AI Evaluation Scientist
positioning.safestPositioning: Enterprise AI quality operations specialist with chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring workflow, A/B testing design, human-review QA loop, and governance automation experience.
positioning.headlineRecommendation: AI Quality Operations / Conversational AI Evaluation Specialist with enterprise chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, and governance automation experience.
whyThisFits: Direct evidence of structured chatbot platform evaluation using benchmark questions, transcript scoring, human review, and live A/B metrics
Direct evidence of automated GPT-based quality evaluation workflow for an internal AI assistant
Direct evidence of live chatbot A/B attribution logic and metric definitions
Direct evidence of AI governance workflow automation and risk classification
Production AI chatbot operations evidence across Global and Japan consumer support markets
hiddenSkillsToSurface: Python/SQL-style pipeline inspection and validation as maintenance, not from-scratch engineering
AI coding tool assisted debugging as interview context only
evidenceToSuppress: evi-ai-coding-tool-assisted-debugging: interview only, not strong visible CV proof
evi-research-lab-management: too weak for this JD's scientist requirement
Raw TOMO, FIN, Trender Buddy, and internal benchmark score names should be replaced with external-friendly wording
claimsToAvoid: AI Evaluation Scientist with production ML ownership
PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow practical ownership
interviewRiskQuestions: Explain the statistical design behind your A/B testing work, including significance, confidence intervals, and effect sizes
How did you calibrate GPT scoring against human judgment?
What benchmark design principles did you use beyond a question set?
What Python code did you personally build versus inspect or coordinate?
Have you used PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow in practical evaluation work?
How would you design a multi-turn agent evaluation harness with tool-use scenarios?
How would you detect hallucination, contamination, and policy violations at scale?

managerIntent.actualJobToBeDone: Build a scientific and production-ready evaluation function that prevents low-quality or unsafe AI-agent behavior from reaching enterprise customers.
hiringManagerPainPoints: AI products are scaling quickly and need reliable quality gates
Existing product teams likely need more scientific evaluation methods than ad hoc testing
AI agents introduce multi-turn, tool-use, long-context, and safety risks
Enterprise customers require quality, safety, reproducibility, and accountability
The organization needs evaluation signals that R&D teams can act on
successSignals: Evaluation pipelines run reliably and reproducibly
Benchmarks and rubrics catch regressions before release
Quality reports clearly drive product improvements
Metrics are statistically defensible and not just subjective QA scores
Safety, hallucination, and policy compliance checks reduce release risk
dealBreakers: No real ML or LLM evaluation implementation experience
Cannot code or maintain Python evaluation pipelines
No practical statistics or experimental design ability
No ML framework exposure where required
Only manual QA or business analysis experience
Cannot communicate in business-level English or fluent Japanese
overqualificationRisks: Candidate may be seen as business/operations-focused rather than research-engineering focused
Power Platform and HR automation evidence may distract from the ML evaluation scientist brief
Too much stakeholder/process wording may make the CV look non-technical for this JD

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: 3+ years practical experience as ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Research Engineer, or ML/AI evaluation-related role
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Recruiter will look for formal ML/data science/evaluation job titles or deep hands-on ML evaluation ownership.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: AI quality evaluation operations for enterprise chatbot and internal AI assistant workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Candidate titles and evidence do not clearly show ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Research Engineer, or formal ML evaluation scientist experience.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Research or implementation experience in ML model evaluation or LLM evaluation
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on LLM evaluation design, metrics, datasets, and reproducible evaluation methods.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Built practical GPT/chatbot evaluation workflows combining scoring, human review, and metric tracking.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence supports operational evaluation but not formal ML model evaluation research.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Deep knowledge of benchmark design, LLM-as-Judge, quantitative quality measurement, and hallucination detection
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can discuss rigorous benchmarks, judge calibration, hallucination methods, and quantitative validity.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Structured benchmark-style chatbot evaluation and GPT-based scoring workflow.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of LLM-as-Judge calibration, hallucination detection methodology, construct validity, or contamination detection.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Statistics and experimental design including hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals, and effect sizes
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can design and interpret statistical experiments, not just route traffic or track metrics.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Designed live A/B routing and reporting logic for chatbot comparison.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Evidence does not prove hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, effect sizes, or statistical significance analysis.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Experience building ML or evaluation pipelines in Python
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on Python engineering for evaluation pipeline design, execution, validation, and maintenance.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Built GPT-based evaluation workflow and inspected existing Python/SQL-style automation logic for validation and troubleshooting.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Do not claim from-scratch Python ML pipeline engineering unless source can prove code ownership.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Practical experience with PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Hands-on ML framework usage for model/evaluation implementation.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No supported evidence for PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow.

jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can design task success, factuality, win rate, rubric, harm, calibration, or other domain-specific metrics.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Defined chatbot quality metrics, GPT scoring categories, human review loop, and A/B reporting measures.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No proof of proper scoring rules, win rate modeling, factuality calibration, or harm metrics.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: AI safety, Responsible AI, red teaming, adversarial testing, and policy compliance verification
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can build safety and compliance verification frameworks for AI systems.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Translated AI/privacy governance requirements into executable risk scoring, routing, and reviewer controls.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Governance evidence is strong, but not automated red teaming or adversarial testing.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Multi-turn, tool-use, long-context, or AI-agent evaluation harnesses
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can evaluate complex AI-agent behavior, tool calls, and long-context workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: AI assistant and chatbot quality evaluation workflow experience.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of multi-agent, long-context, or tool-use evaluation harness implementation.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: CI/CD quality gates and scalable automated evaluation infrastructure
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Evaluation checks integrated into release pipelines and production engineering workflows.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Automated quality workflow and production data-pipeline validation support.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No direct evidence of CI/CD quality gate ownership or scalable ML evaluation infrastructure.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Master's degree or equivalent in related field
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Advanced quantitative, scientific, or technical education.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Master of Biomedical Sciences plus AI/Big Data training and scientific research operations background.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: Degree is scientific but not CS, ML, statistics, mathematics, physics, or psychometrics.

jdEvidenceMapping.requirement: Business-level English or fluent Japanese for product development discussion
jdEvidenceMapping.marketExpectation: Can collaborate technically in English or Japanese without friction.
jdEvidenceMapping.safeCvAngle: Cross-region Global/Japan AI chatbot operations and Philippines Workday KT facilitation.
jdEvidenceMapping.gapOrRisk: No formal language proficiency evidence provided; do not claim fluent Japanese.

internalTerminology.originalTerm: TOMO Consumer Chatbot Evaluation & A/B Testing
internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: customer-service chatbot platform evaluation and A/B testing program
internalTerminology.reason: Internal project name is not meaningful externally and may expose internal naming.
Internal product/platform name needs explanation and is not necessary for screening.
Internal assistant name should be translated into business function.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: benchmark question set for chatbot answer quality comparison
internalTerminology.reason: The concept is useful but should be explained as a benchmark-style evaluation set.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: enterprise GenAI enablement and milestone tracking hub
internalTerminology.reason: Internal hub name should be replaced by its function.

internalTerminology.originalTerm: Legal DPIA App / AI Impact Assessment
internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: privacy and AI risk assessment workflow application
internalTerminology.reason: External readers need the compliance function more than the internal app name.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: learning data synchronization job into analytics data layer
internalTerminology.reason: Internal job name is too specific and not suitable for CV wording.

internalTerminology.externalFriendlyWording: automated recruiting alert for long-open requisitions with no candidate pipeline
internalTerminology.reason: Internal workflow name should be converted into business outcome.

remainingGaps.gap: No supported PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow experience
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not list these tools. If applying, complete and document a small LLM evaluation notebook using one framework or state no direct framework experience in interview.

remainingGaps.gap: Limited proof of advanced statistics: hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, effect sizes, significance testing
remainingGaps.mitigation: CV should only claim A/B routing and metric definition. Prepare an interview explanation of what was measured and avoid claiming statistical inference unless documented.

remainingGaps.gap: No direct reward modeling, RLHF, DPO, proper scoring rules, IRT, construct validity, or contamination detection evidence
remainingGaps.mitigation: Do not include these as skills. Treat them as learning gaps and ask whether the role can accept AI quality operations background.

remainingGaps.gap: Python evaluation pipeline ownership is only partially supported
remainingGaps.mitigation: Frame Python as existing pipeline inspection, validation, and troubleshooting unless source confirms from-scratch pipeline code ownership.

remainingGaps.gap: No CI/CD evaluation quality gate ownership
remainingGaps.mitigation: Use production monitoring and automated GPT evaluation workflow language, not CI/CD gate ownership.

remainingGaps.gap: Language requirement is not fully evidenced
remainingGaps.mitigation: State only verified business communication languages and avoid claiming fluent Japanese.

qualityTargets.weakness: Candidate is not clearly an AI Evaluation Scientist or ML research engineer.
qualityTargets.improvement: Use AI Evaluation Operations or Conversational AI Quality Operations positioning, not scientist title.

qualityTargets.weakness: Has chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, AI governance, Python mention, and production AI ops, but lacks ML framework and research keywords.
qualityTargets.improvement: Add supported exact terms: LLM evaluation, GPT scoring, chatbot evaluation framework, A/B testing, human review, AI quality workflow.

qualityTargets.weakness: Major gaps in ML frameworks, statistics, research-level evaluation, and CI/CD quality gates.
qualityTargets.improvement: Apply only as stretch or build supplemental project proof before applying.

qualityTargets.weakness: Practical AI evaluation operations evidence is strong, but not science-level evaluation infrastructure.
qualityTargets.improvement: Lead with 3-4 strongest AI evaluation examples and suppress HRIS-heavy evidence.

qualityTargets.weakness: High temptation to overstate GPT scoring as LLM-as-Judge calibration or Python automation as ML pipeline engineering.
qualityTargets.improvement: Keep claims conservative and explicitly operational.

coreScreeningThresholds: Can this person build or maintain Python-based ML/LLM evaluation pipelines?
Can this person explain LLM evaluation science, not just chatbot QA?
Can this person design statistically valid experiments and interpret results?
Can this person work with ML frameworks such as PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow?
Can this person create benchmarks, rubrics, hallucination checks, and safety evaluations?
Can this person operate in English or Japanese technical product discussions?
hardRequirements: 3+ years in ML, data science, research engineering, or ML/AI evaluation roles
LLM or ML evaluation implementation experience

ambiguousSignals.jdPhrase: Lead the design, construction, and operation
ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: They expect ownership from methodology through production infrastructure, not only coordination.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Use built, defined, automated, validated, and operated only where evidence supports those verbs.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: They want rigor, statistical validity, reproducibility, and defensible metrics.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Show benchmark questions, transcript scoring, human review validation, A/B metrics, and quality reports, but avoid claiming formal evaluation science.

ambiguousSignals.jdPhrase: last line of defense for release quality
ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: High accountability for preventing bad AI releases.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Emphasize production AI chatbot operations, quality workflows, issue tracking, and governance controls.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: Can communicate technical evaluation results with product and engineering teams.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Mention cross-region Global/Japan operations and cross-functional stakeholders only if language level can be verified.

ambiguousSignals.likelyScreeningMeaning: They need ambiguity tolerance and method-building, not just following an existing checklist.
ambiguousSignals.howToShowItInCv: Show creation of evaluation frameworks, A/B metric definitions, and governance workflows from ambiguous requirements.

likelyInterviewQuestionThemes: Design an LLM-as-Judge evaluation and explain calibration against human reviewers
Explain how you would build a benchmark for a multi-turn tool-using AI agent
Explain your A/B testing design and how you would determine statistical significance
Walk through a Python evaluation pipeline you personally built or maintained
Describe how you detect hallucination, bias, harm, or policy non-compliance
Explain how your GPT scoring workflow handled rubrics, categories, and human review
Discuss what you did directly versus what engineers implemented
Explain whether you have used PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow, LangSmith, or Weights & Biases
mustHaveSignals: Visible A/B testing and metric definition evidence
Visible GPT scoring and human review loop evidence
Visible production AI chatbot operations evidence
Clear education signal with Master's degree, without overstating CS/ML specialization
summaryAngle: Stretch application. The CV should not try to look like a pure AI Evaluation Scientist. The strongest safe angle is enterprise AI quality operations: chatbot evaluation framework, GPT scoring workflow, A/B attribution metrics, production AI chatbot operations, and AI governance controls. Major ML/statistics/framework gaps must remain visible internally so the CV generator does not overclaim.
recommendedNextAction: Apply only as a stretch if the CV is positioned as AI quality evaluation operations. Before applying, add a short project or portfolio note proving LLM evaluation methodology, Python evaluation script ownership, rubric design, human-judge calibration, and statistical analysis basics; otherwise prioritize adjacent AI Product Ops or Conversational AI Quality roles.

transferableStrengths.strength: AI quality evaluation operations for enterprise chatbot and internal AI assistant workflows.

Built GPT-based evaluation workflow and inspected existing Python/SQL-style automation logic for validation and troubleshooting.
Defined chatbot quality metrics, GPT scoring categories, human review loop, and A/B reporting measures.
Translated AI/privacy governance requirements into executable risk scoring, routing, and reviewer controls.
Master of Biomedical Sciences plus AI/Big Data training and scientific research operations background.
Cross-region Global/Japan AI chatbot operations and Philippines Workday KT facilitation.

recommendedPositioning.headline: AI Quality Operations / Conversational AI Evaluation Specialist with enterprise chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring, A/B testing, and governance automation experience.
recommendedPositioning.summaryAngle: Stretch application. The CV should not try to look like a pure AI Evaluation Scientist. The strongest safe angle is enterprise AI quality operations: chatbot evaluation framework, GPT scoring workflow, A/B attribution metrics, production AI chatbot operations, and AI governance controls. Major ML/statistics/framework gaps must remain visible internally so the CV generator does not overclaim.
wordingGuidance: Enterprise AI quality operations specialist with chatbot evaluation, GPT scoring workflow, A/B testing design, human-review QA loop, and governance automation experience.
Use adjacent-fit wording and explain weak evidence as risk.
Strengthen wording only when supported by selected evidence.
Preserve unsupported requirements as gaps or risks, not visible strengths.
